item.title: Inpex targets two carbon capture wells in Japan this year
item.description: Japanese operator progressing carbon capture projects, both domestic and international, to reach emissions intensity targets by 2035

item.title: Oil prices, stocks plummet as questions surround US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: US majors see shares dip as Brent, WTI fall to lowest close in two weeks

item.title: ExxonMobil anticipates higher first quarter earnings despite Middle East disruptions
item.description: Oil price surge could boost US supermajor’s haul by $2.3 billion, but a $500 million production hit looms

item.title: Santos eyes new US oilfields development
item.description: Production from Quokka-Mitquq could rival Pikka Phase 1, which is now expected on stream within weeks

item.title: ‘We are like the subsea NASA’: The R&D hub shaping Petrobras’ future
item.description: Petrobras' Cenpes developing several technologies to improve exploration and production activities

item.title: Canada junior's stock price up 22% on Namibia deal
item.description: Oregen Energy signs letter of intent that could see it boost Orange basin exposure

item.title: McDermott confident of navigating Middle East crisis
item.description: US company is a world-leading EPCI contractor with a big footprint in the Middle East

item.title: Shell prays for Namibia success as it drills critical exploration well
item.description: Supermajor hopes Merlin-1X well will discover much needed oil reserves in 'Millenium Star' reservoir

item.title: Fresh fire breaks out as Adnoc's largest gas facility remains shutdown
item.description: Habshan complex in southwest Abu Dhabi is one of the largest gas processing facilities in the world and is operated by Adnoc Gas, a subsidiary of Adnoc

item.title: Santos facing another 10 days to restart production at flagship gas project
item.description: Teething troubles on Barossa FPSO offshore Australia have prolonged offshore commissioning

item.title: FPSO player loses its CEO
item.description: Gary Christenson has stepped down from Bumi Armada with immediate effect

item.title: Iran-linked cyberattack targets critical field logic controllers
item.description: US government notice warns oil and gas firms of an ongoing cyberattack on operational technology controllers

item.title: Oil and gas players warned of Iran-linked cyberattack
item.description: US government notice warns oil and gas companies of an ongoing cyberattack on operational technology controllers

item.title: China’s national oil companies partner for offshore hydrocarbon development
item.description: Subsidiaries of Chinese NOCs sign strategic cooperation agreement to expand oil and gas exploration

item.title: Chinese state players team up for offshore oil and gas development
item.description: Subsidiaries of Chinese state players sign cooperation agreement to expand oil and gas exploration

item.title: Saipem and Technip Energies line up yards for Inpex’s Abadi FPSO
item.description: As FEED work on Inpex’s $20 billion Abadi LNG project nears completion, rival consortia finalise subcontractor deals ahead of 2027 final investment decision

item.title: Trump agrees two-week ceasefire with Iran, subject to Hormuz opening
item.description: US President said cessation of hostilities 'based on conversations with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir, of Pakistan'

item.title: Trump agrees two-week ceasefire with Iran, subject to Strait of Hormuz opening
item.description: US president said cessation of hostilities 'based on conversations with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir, of Pakistan'

item.title: WTI trades above Brent as Trump’s Strait of Hormuz deadline approaches
item.description: Brent settles near $107, while WTI stops around $112

item.title: Petronas joins forces with Subsea7 and OneSubsea for Suriname developments
item.description: Malaysian company eyeing final investment decision at Block 52 later this year

item.title: Brent crude to average $115 a barrel in second quarter amid Middle East shut-ins: EIA
item.description: Output cuts could rise to 9.1 million barrels per day in April

item.title: Brent crude to average $115 per barrel in second quarter amid Middle East shut-ins: EIA
item.description: Output cuts could rise to 9.1 million barrels per day in April

item.title: US LNG developer seals offtake contracts as final investment decision looms
item.description: Commonwealth LNG says customers include EQT, Petronas, Aramco

item.title: Louisiana governor sees progress on carbon capture well permits after application pause
item.description: Jeff Landry says three more Class VI authorisations possible by end of 2026

item.title: Touchstone aims to quadruple production after ‘wiping out’ competition in Trinidad & Tobago
item.description: Chief executive Paul Baay tells Upstream the Canadian independent seeks to achieve output of 20,000 boepd

item.title: Seadrill wins $480 million rig contract extension with Petrobras
item.description: Drillship West Polaris will continue drilling offshore Brazil until early 2031

item.title: Key details revealed as Argentina LNG progresses towards end-of-year FID
item.description: YPC CEO told Upstream economics will make project resilient against market volatility

item.title: Eni makes big gas and condensate find in East Mediterranean
item.description: Denise West discovery estimated to hold 2 Tcf of gas and 130 million barrels of liquids

item.title: ExxonMobil gears up for major new Cyprus gas project
item.description: Declaration of commerciality filed for Glaucus and Pegasus discoveries, with appraisal plans afoot

item.title: UK North Sea operator making moves to secure rig requirements
item.description: Harbour has Paul B Loyd on contract with multiple options
